---
title: "between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
tbl-colwidths: [30, 8, 22, 22, 26]  # aa-col-added
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-codon log2 fold-change of day_10-vs-day_0 median per-replicate frequency, with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint, across the 61 sense codons. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched at day_10 (log2FC ≥ 0), red = depleted at day_10 (log2FC < 0). Y-axis range ~−1.3 to +1.5. P-site GGG (~+1.15) and E-site ATA (~+1.40) are the largest day_10 enrichments; tail-end depletions at A-site TTA (~−1.20), P-site CTA (~−1.24), and E-site TTA / GCG (~−1.2) all sit in `low-count` rare-codon territory.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/codon/composite/APE_codon_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 61 sense codons by signed log2_FC (day_10 median / day_0 median) at one ribosome site. Bars above zero are day_10-enriched relative to day_0; bars below zero are day_10-depleted. The extreme |log2_FC| values in each panel's tails are concentrated on rare codons with median frequencies < 0.5% — read them as `low-count` exploratory candidates rather than headline shifts.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`, `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`, `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`, `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`, `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided) (source: user-confirmed)
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 (BWM_d10_rep2/3 + control_d10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_d0_rep2/3 + control_d0_rep2/3); BWM and control reps pooled inside each timepoint; two-sided MW on per-replicate codon frequencies; BH-FDR per E/P/A site (each site = own family of 61 sense codons); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_d10 / median_d0).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor. Empirically: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in any of the 6 files. Proposed by Dylan, confirmed by user in clarification round 2.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control reps; if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design. Proposed by Dylan, confirmed by user in clarification round 2.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 MW has very low power; null results across all 6 files are weakly informative. Proposed by Dylan, confirmed by user in clarification round 2.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ≈ 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative. Proposed by Dylan, confirmed by user in clarification round 2.

---

## Headline

**Firm null at FDR<0.05 (0/183) for codon-level d10-vs-d0 MW with BWM and control reps pooled per timepoint; min raw p = 0.0286 (n=4 vs n=4 floor), 3 floor rows total — E-site TCC (+0.589), P-site GAG (+0.387), P-site TGC (+0.219). Min p_adj = 0.498 at A site (no floor row, but a 14-codon block tied at raw p=0.114 produces the BH wall). 'No FDR hits' is structural per the locked floor caveat, not a biological negative.**

Firm null at FDR<0.05 (0/183) for codon-level day_10 vs day_0 MW with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint (n=4 per side). Three floor rows in the whole file: E-TCC (+0.589), P-GAG (+0.387), P-TGC (+0.219). Min p_adj = 0.498 at site A — driven by a 14-codon block tied at raw p = 0.114, producing the BH wall 0.114 × 61/14 ≈ 0.498. Site A has the densest near-floor signal but no floor rows; sites E and P each have 1–2 floor rows but min p_adj climbs to 0.93 / 0.69. Treat as exploratory leads only; the per-timepoint Fisher (`per_timepoint_fisher_codon`) at day_0 and day_10 is the test that can actually resolve a BWM-vs-control codon shift at either timepoint.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (day_10 / day_0 median ratio). `p_adj` is BH-corrected per E/P/A site (family of 61 codons). Each Dylan site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means day_10-enriched; negative means day_10-depleted. Row order is Dylan's source order. Dylan's group order is preserved (A is the headline group; E and P follow). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

### A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ------------ |
| CGC   | R   | +0.640             | 0.498                | nominal-only |
| AAC   | N   | +0.595             | 0.498                | nominal-only |
| ATC   | I   | +0.518             | 0.498                | nominal-only |
| ACC   | T   | +0.295             | 0.498                | nominal-only |
| CGT   | R   | +0.276             | 0.498                | nominal-only |

### A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| TTA   | L   | −1.201             | 0.498                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GCA   | A   | −1.065             | 0.498                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GTG   | V   | −0.901             | 0.498                | nominal-only, low-count |
| CTG   | L   | −0.800             | 0.498                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GGC   | G   | −0.639             | 0.498                | nominal-only, low-count |

### E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag  |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ----- |
| TCC   | S   | +0.589             | 0.930                | floor |
| CTC   | L   | +0.490             | 0.930                |       |
| CAG   | Q   | +0.267             | 0.930                |       |
| TGC   | C   | +0.220             | 0.930                |       |
| GCT   | A   | +0.113             | 0.930                |       |

### E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| GCG   | A   | −1.204             | 0.930                | nominal-only, low-count |
| AGG   | R   | −0.767             | 0.930                | low-count               |
| CGA   | R   | −0.678             | 0.930                |                         |
| AAA   | K   | −0.468             | 0.930                |                         |
| ATG   | M   | −0.384             | 0.930                |                         |

### P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ------------ |
| GAG   | E   | +0.387             | 0.688                | floor        |
| TGC   | C   | +0.219             | 0.688                | floor        |
| CCC   | P   | +0.298             | 0.688                | nominal-only |
| GCC   | A   | +0.221             | 0.688                | nominal-only |
| GGG   | G   | +1.147             | 0.688                | low-count    |

### P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_0)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag                    |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | -------------------- | ----------------------- |
| CTA   | L   | −1.242             | 0.688                | low-count               |
| GCG   | A   | −1.031             | 0.688                | nominal-only, low-count |
| GCA   | A   | −0.698             | 0.688                |                         |
| ATT   | I   | −0.578             | 0.688                |                         |
| GGT   | G   | −0.146             | 0.688                |                         |

### Flag glossary

- `floor` — the row sits at the test's discrete minimum possible raw p-value, given the sample size. For MW two-sided at n=4 vs n=4 this floor is exactly 0.02857. BH-correction over a 61-codon per-site family puts the wall for a single floor hit at ≈ 1.74 (so a lone floor hit cannot even reach `p_adj` < 1); two floor hits → 0.85; three → 0.57; FDR<0.05 cannot be reached without near-universal tying at the floor across a site.
- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` ≥ 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family.
- `low-count` — the codon's count or median frequency is below the test's stability threshold; the `log2_FC` column for this row is dominated by single-rep fluctuations and should not be over-read. At codon level the rare-codon median frequencies in this file include TTA, CTA, AGG, CGG, GCG, ATA all below ~0.5%.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 183
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.498 (A site, 14-codon block tied at raw p = 0.114)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02857 (= MW exact floor); 3 rows at the floor (E-TCC +0.589, P-GAG +0.387, P-TGC +0.219)
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 two-sided exact)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.498 (no floor rows)
  - E site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.930 (1 floor row)
  - P site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.688 (2 floor rows)

## Biological interpretation

**The file is a firm null at FDR<0.05 across 183 codon-level day_10-vs-day_0 comparisons, and the null is structural rather than biological (concrete).** Raw-p is clamped at 0.02857 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor) and the 61-codon per-site BH family puts the minimum achievable `p_adj` for a single floor hit at 1.74 — i.e. no single floor row can even individually move below `p_adj` = 1. The observed picture (3 floor rows file-wide, 2 of them at the P-site) is the design's structural ceiling. The aa sister had min `p_adj` = 0.571 with one floor row; codon resolution actually has a lower file-min `p_adj` (0.498) but driven by a 14-codon tied block at the A-site rather than by floor rows — both readings point at the same structural blockage.

**Aa-vs-codon agreement at the P-site exposes codon-usage shifts rather than amino-acid shifts (exploratory).** Two of the AA sister's closest-to-significant P-site cells are carried entirely by one synonym each at codon resolution. P-aa C (+0.405, floor in the aa sister) is GCT in this file — but specifically TGC (+0.219, floor) carries the signal while TGT is essentially flat at +0.04 (not in the top hits). P-aa E (+0.373, near-floor) is split unevenly across its two synonyms: GAG (+0.387, floor) carries it while GAA (+0.02) is flat. These are codon-usage shifts rather than amino-acid shifts, and the codon-resolution view is more informative than the aa sister for both cells. Neither survives FDR, but both are coherent exploratory leads.

**E-site TCC is a single-codon floor row with no clear aa-sister analogue (exploratory).** E-TCC (+0.589, floor) is one of 6 Ser codons; the aa sister had E-S at a flat +0.18 (not in the top hits). If TCC at the E-site is a real day_10 enrichment, it is a synonymous-codon-specific signature on Ser that the AA resolution averages out. The mechanistic anchor for a single Ser codon at the E-site is not obvious from the report's domain knowledge — this is most naturally read as a discrete-floor candidate to triangulate against the per-timepoint Fisher rather than to elevate to a finding.

**A-site shows a densely tied near-floor block but no floor rows; almost all extreme |log2_FC| cells are `low-count` (exploratory).** A-site's `p_adj` floor (0.498) sits below the P-site and E-site floors, but is built from a 14-codon tied block at raw p = 0.114, not from any cell at the n=4 MW floor. The 5 largest-|effect| A-site cells in the depleted direction (TTA −1.20, GCA −1.07, GTG −0.90, CTG −0.80, GGC −0.64) are all `low-count`; their |log2_FC| values are dominated by sampling noise, not mechanism. The 5 largest-|effect| enriched cells (CGC, AAC, ATC, ACC, CGT) are more stable but none is mechanistically anchored individually. Cross-check whether any of these reappear at the day_0 or day_10 timepoint in `per_timepoint_fisher_codon`.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file is null at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_0 at codon resolution with conditions pooled; the null is structural under MW n=4 vs n=4 with a 61-codon BH family. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does P-TGC (the floor row carrying the P-aa C signal) reappear at day_0 vs day_10 in `per_timepoint_fisher_codon` or in `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon` with BWM vs control resolved? (2) does P-GAG (the floor row carrying the P-aa E signal) show the same codon-usage shift in the within-condition Fisher contrasts? (3) does E-TCC (the orphan floor row with no aa-sister anchor) reproduce anywhere? Noise to ignore: every `low-count` row with |log2_FC| > 0.6 (TTA, GCA, GTG, CTG, GGC at A; GCG, AGG at E; CTA, GCG, GGG at P) — the magnitudes are not real numbers to quote.

## Methods

Dylan proposed Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate codon frequencies, n=4 day_10 (BWM_day10_rep2/3 + control_day10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_0 (BWM_day0_rep2/3 + control_day0_rep2/3), BH-FDR per site (each E/P/A site = 61-codon family); user confirmed. Effect column is `log2_FC` of medians; test statistic is `U_stat`. The test answers "do day_10 and day_0 reps differ in per-rep codon frequency at this site?", with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint. Does *not* answer "BWM-vs-control at any single day" (that is `per_timepoint_fisher_codon`) and does *not* answer "BWM moves from day_0 to day_10 holding condition fixed" (that is `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon`).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** Raw-p is clamped at 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 floor); for a 61-codon per-site BH family, even a lone floor hit BH-corrects to ≈ 1.74 — i.e. no single floor row can clear `p_adj` < 1 by itself. "0/183 at FDR<0.05" here is the design's structural ceiling, not biological evidence that day_10 ≈ day_0.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each timepoint's n=4 is 2 BWM + 2 control; opposite-direction BWM and control time responses cancel inside this MW. Use `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon` to disentangle.
- **n=4-low-power.** Low power; null is weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** Headline above explicitly flags 0.0286 as the raw-p floor and the structural source of "no FDR hits".
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` of medians only; rare codons (TTA, CTA, GCG ~0.001 median freq) get the same column real estate as common codons (AAG, AAA ~0.05) and dominate the |effect| tails.
- **larger-bh-family.** Per-site BH family expands from 20 (aa) to 61 (codon); the BH wall for a single floor hit moves from ~0.57 (aa) to ~1.74 (codon). The codon resolution is structurally further from FDR<0.05 than the aa resolution for the same data, despite finer-grained dynamic range in raw p.
- **low-count-rare-codon-instability.** The top |effect| ranks in this file are dominated by codons with median freq < 0.5% (TTA, GCA, GTG, CTG at A; GCG, AGG at E; CTA, GCG, GGG, TGT/TGC small-count at P). Their `log2_FC` magnitudes are not stable across small sample fluctuations and should be read as sampling noise, not biology.

### Considered and ruled out

*(Dylan did not propose any further per-CSV caveats here.)*

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CGC, AAC, ATC, GGA, ACC, CGT, AAT, GAT, GTC, TGT, ...; right: TTG, CCT, GGC, ATA, TAT, CTG, GCG, CTA, GTG, GCA, CGA, TTA). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~−1.2 to +0.7. Largest enrichment at CGC (~+0.64); largest depletion at TTA (~−1.20). Tail-end depletions are concentrated on rare codons.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_A_codon_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: GGG, TGT, TCC, GTC, GAG, ACC, CCC, CGG, AAC, GCC, ...; right: AGG, TTG, CCT, ATG, ATT, TGG, GCA, ACG, GCG, CTA). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~−1.3 to +1.2. Largest enrichment at GGG (~+1.15, `low-count`); largest depletion at CTA (~−1.24, `low-count`). Two floor rows visible at GAG and TGC (not at the extreme magnitudes — floor flag tracks raw-p, not effect size).](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_P_codon_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_0 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: ATA, TCC, CTC, CAA, TGT, CGC, CAG, TCT, TGC, GGA, ...; right: CCG, GTG, CTA, CGA, TAT, AGG, CGG, GCG, TTA). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~−1.3 to +1.4. Largest enrichment at ATA (~+1.38, `low-count`); largest depletions at TTA, GCG, CGG (~−1.0 to −1.2, all `low-count`). One floor row visible mid-pack at TCC.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/site_E_codon_Day10_vs_Day0_barplot.png)
